name: Navalagamella, Zarzalejo, and Valdemorillo Loop
name_original: Navalagamella-Zarzalejo-Valdemorillo – circular por Cañal Cojo y Camino de la Crucijada
description: A moderate 22.7-mile mountain bike loop near Navalagamella, featuring historical sites, scenic views, and varied terrain.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Navalagamella are situated in the northwest of the Community of Madrid, characterized by diverse landscapes ideal for cycling. The region features varied terrain, including mountains, lush nature, and river valleys such as the Perales River. Riders can expect a mix of steep climbs, rapid descents, and technical sections, often weaving through oak forests and past historical sites. This geographical setting provides a range of mountain biking experiences for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Navalagamella The most popular mountain bike trail is Navalagamella, Zarzalejo, and Valdemorillo Loop, a 22.7 miles (36.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic vistas and integrates historical sites. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Old Lime Kiln of Quijorna – Vado del Río Perales loop from Navalagamella, a moderate 24.2 miles (39.0 km) path. This route explores the natural beauty around the Perales River, passing through serene oak forests. Local mountain bikers also love the Viewpoint of the city of Madrid – Navahonda Hermitage loop from Navalagamella, a 28.5 miles (45.9 km) trail leading through elevated terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Mountain biking around Navalagamella is defined by mountains, river valleys, and oak forests, offering a mix of natural beauty and historical elements. The network provides options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Navalagamella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navalagamella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Navalagamella?
A: Navalagamella offers a selection of over 20 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with more than 250 mountain bikers using komoot to discover the area's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails in Navalagamella?
A: The trails in Navalagamella feature diverse terrain, ranging from mountains and lush natural areas to river valleys, particularly along the Perales River. Riders can anticipate a mix of steep climbs, rapid descents, and technical sections, often winding through serene oak forests and past historical sites. The region's varied landscape provides an engaging experience for mountain bikers.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for beginners or families in Navalagamella?
A: While the komoot guide primarily features moderate to difficult mountain bike routes, the region of Navalagamella generally offers varied terrain. For those seeking a less challenging experience, the area around the Cerro Alarcón Reservoir is known for an easy 3.8-mile circular route, though specific easy komoot tours are not highlighted in this guide.

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike routes in Navalagamella?
A: For experienced riders looking for a challenge, Navalagamella offers several difficult routes. One such option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19212112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the city of Madrid – Navahonda Hermitage loop from Navalagamella&lt;/a&gt;, a 28.5-mile trail that leads through elevated terrain and typically takes around 4 hours 20 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Navalagamella?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Navalagamella are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962685776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalagamella, Zarzalejo, and Valdemorillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 22.7-mile moderate route that offers scenic vistas and incorporates historical sites.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Navalagamella's trails often integrate natural beauty with historical elements. You might encounter historical mills along the Perales River, such as the well-preserved Molino Alto, or a unique bridge over the river. The region also features an ancient juniper tree, estimated to be between 3 and 4 centuries old. For broader exploration, nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5155198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmayor Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4318799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of San Lorenzo de El Escorial&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that follow the Perales River?
A: Yes, the Perales River is a prominent feature in the region, and several routes explore its natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3975165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Lime Kiln of Quijorna – Vado del Río Perales loop from Navalagamella&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 24.2-mile path that specifically explores the area around the Perales River, passing through serene oak forests.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Navalagamella?
A: The mountain bike routes in Navalagamella are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.3 stars from more than 5 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the variety of terrain that offers both challenging climbs and enjoyable descents.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navalagamella?
A: Navalagamella, located in the Community of Madrid, generally experiences a Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Is it possible to reach Navalagamella by public transport for mountain biking?
A: Navalagamella is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services from Madrid. Cyclists should check the specific bus lines and their policies regarding bicycle transport, as this can vary. Planning ahead will ensure a smooth journey to the starting points of the trails.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting Navalagamella for mountain biking?
A: Navalagamella, as a municipality, offers various parking options within the town. Specific parking areas for trailheads may vary, but generally, you can find street parking or designated public parking spaces in and around the town center, providing convenient access to the local trail network.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Navalagamella offers routes that extend for significant distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5307226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalagamella – Cruceiro Monument loop from Navalagamella&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate route spanning approximately 48 miles (77 km), providing an extended exploration of the region.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails in Navalagamella?
A: While the region of Navalagamella is generally dog-friendly, specific rules for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary. It's always advisable to keep dogs under control, preferably on a leash, and to check for any local signage or regulations regarding pets in natural areas or protected zones to ensure a respectful and safe experience for everyone.
tips: Very close to the center of Navalagamella is this beautiful viewpoint with the best views of Alarcón and beyond Madrid. It deserves to visit it and have it on the path of your route.

Bunker of the rebel side in position "Cota 960" during the Spanish Civil War, this structure has five marksman posts and a covered machine gun nest, all with very good visibility at the time (the current vegetation was not there). These were connected by a single covered gallery with a single entrance and several shooting loopholes.
Fresnedillas was under Franco's rule since the fall of 1936.
Wide and just a short stretch of sand. It has some costs but above all good views. I have found some cows along the way.
Nice rock formation. There are stairs that allow you to climb to enjoy the view
Very picturesque track and in perfect condition. It is not technically difficult and it is a good option to avoid any trials with loose rock and comfortably reach Zarzalejo.
Another of the good tracks that surrounds the Zarzalejo area. With beautiful views, this route will take you to Valdemorillo along a very picturesque path.
Easy track to ride. In rainy seasons you will have to cross a river that can be avoided by a small bridge.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route offers a varied terrain typical of the Madrid Sierra foothills. You'll encounter sections of dirt tracks, some rocky paths, and potentially loose gravel, especially on descents. The route includes named sections like Camino de Cañal Cojo and Colada del Camino de la Crucijada, which are generally well-defined but can have technical spots. Expect some climbs and descents, as indicated by the moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and can be started from several points in Navalagamella, Zarzalejo, or Valdemorillo. For convenient access, look for public parking areas within these towns. Specific parking details are not provided, but starting from Navalagamella often offers good options.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, this route is generally not recommended for absolute beginners. While it doesn't feature extreme technical sections, the distance of over 36 km and the cumulative elevation gain of nearly 550 meters require a good level of physical fitness and some mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several interesting spots. Keep an eye out for the View of Cerro Alarcón and Madrid, the historical Fresnedillas Civil War Bunker, and the unique Rock Formation with Stairs in the Dehesa de Robledo. The route also passes by the Vado del Río Perales, offering a scenic river crossing.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking this route. The temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially midday, so if you ride then, start early. Winters can be chilly, but clear days are often pleasant.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on this route. However, as parts of the trail pass through natural areas and potentially near livestock, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in the Dehesa sections, to ensure their safety and respect wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any specific permits or have to pay entrance fees to ride this mountain bike route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for mountain biking in the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del río Guadarrama?
faqs.answer: Yes, as this route is within the Parque Regional del Curso Medio del río Guadarrama y su entorno, it's important to follow general park regulations. This includes staying on marked trails, respecting wildlife, not littering, and being mindful of other users. Mountain biking is generally permitted on designated paths, but always check for any temporary restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Cañada R. Leonesa, Cordel del Puente de San Juan, and the Vereda del Camino de Robledo de Chavela. It also briefly touches parts of the GR10 long-distance path.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near the towns of Navalagamella, Zarzalejo, and Valdemorillo. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, or shops where you can refuel and rehydrate. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your ride, especially between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain bike ride, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, sufficient water (at least 2-3 liters), energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, multi-tool), and appropriate clothing for the weather. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended, especially during sunny periods.
